'''Andy Bracek''' (born 21 March 1987) is a [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf|pr}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Bracek was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester]], England.

==Playing career==
He played at representative level for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]],<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and at club level for [[Leigh Miners Rangers]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] (two spells) and [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]].

Bracek attended [[Bedford High School (Leigh)|Bedford High School]] in [[Bedford, Greater Manchester|Bedford]], [[Leigh, Greater Manchester|Leigh]], leaving in 2003. Throughout his childhood, Bracek trained and played at [[Leigh Miners Rangers]], progressing through the ranks until [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] signed him to a youth deal as a teenager.

In November 2004, Bracek handed in a written transfer request at St. Helens and would go on to sign for Warrington for a fee of £20,000.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/6676649.saints-sell-bracek/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230209141235/https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/6676649.saints-sell-bracek/ |archive-date=9 February 2023 |title=Saints sell Bracek |publisher=St Helens Star |date=11 November 2004 |access-date=9 February 2023}}</ref>

Bracek was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Crusaders dominate Wales' squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Crusaders dominate Wales' squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-03|access-date=2008-10-04|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20081004195348/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|archive-date= 4 October 2008 |url-status= live}}</ref>
